title: Tenant onboarding v2 and pilot setup kit
description: Take the stronger three-outage wedge and make it easier to stand up for a real pilot. Tenant setup should more clearly express supported outage families, owner routing, downstream targets, reviewer expectations, and pack availability so a new customer environment can be prepared with less founder-led interpretation.
implementation_tasks: deepen tenant bootstrap and readiness semantics for real pilot usage
make supported outage families, pack coverage, owner mappings, and delivery targets more explicit in setup surfaces
show missing pilot prerequisites clearly to operators and admins
document the minimum viable pilot setup path for a new tenant
done_when: a new tenant can be prepared for a bounded pilot with less founder intervention
pilot-readiness state is visible and actionable in-product
setup expectations for the three-outage wedge are easier to understand

title: Pilot ROI instrumentation and case-based proof capture
description: Make pilot-value proof more durable than a one-time demo. The product should capture and present case-based proof across the supported outage families so buyer and pilot conversations can rely on structured evidence rather than only narration.
implementation_tasks: deepen ROI and proof capture per tenant and per incident family
expose the product signals needed for case-based buyer proof
strengthen training or proof surfaces around relay reduction, runtime-backed coverage, and handoff completion
keep all value claims aligned with measured or honestly estimated evidence
done_when: pilot-value proof is more durable and less demo-dependent
buyer-proof surfaces reflect the three-outage wedge clearly
product claims remain evidence-backed and bounded

title: Delivery and feedback closure v2
description: Deepen the workflow after a case is handed off so the system feels more complete in repeated pilot use. Delivery, acknowledgment, action outcome, rejection, and follow-up states should be easier to understand and more visibly connected back into product learning.
implementation_tasks: deepen delivery lifecycle and engineering feedback capture
make post-handoff states more complete and auditable
surface acknowledgment, acted-on, rejected, and follow-up states where appropriate
keep the lifecycle connected to memory and outcome capture
done_when: downstream delivery and outcome states feel more complete for repeated workflow use
operators can understand post-handoff progress more clearly
the product better closes the loop from triage to learning

title: Operator workflow simplification and role clarity v2
description: Reduce friction in repeated pilot usage by simplifying the operator path from intake to review to handoff. The product should make role-specific responsibilities and next actions clearer so it is easier for a support team to use NEXUS repeatedly without founder narration.
implementation_tasks: simplify the repeated-use operator path across intake, incident, review, and handoff surfaces
clarify next actions and role-specific visibility where the current workflow still feels heavy
reduce wording and explanation debt on the main operator path
update runbooks and walkthroughs to match the simplified flow
done_when: repeated operator use feels lower-friction
role-specific behavior is clearer in the workflow
the product is easier to use in a real pilot setting

title: Repo-aware debugger handoff deepening
description: Make the engineering handoff feel more technically actionable by deepening repo-aware trace cues without overclaiming universal debugging. The goal is to improve likely owner, likely module, likely inspection point, and 'inspect here first' quality for bounded incidents.
implementation_tasks: strengthen trace-to-repo cues such as likely module, likely owner, and likely inspection boundary
improve the 'inspect here first' packet without implying arbitrary repository coverage
make the engineering packet more useful for a real engineering reviewer
keep the bounded nature of the debugger story explicit
done_when: engineering handoff packets feel more repo-aware and inspection-ready
trace packets are more actionable without overclaiming debugging breadth
the debugger story becomes more technically convincing to engineering buyers

title: Runtime evidence weighting and mitigation confidence v2
description: Deepen how runtime evidence influences mitigation posture so the product feels more decision-driving. Recommendations should more clearly distinguish inferred best actions, runtime-improved actions, and runtime-resolved actions, with stronger residual-risk framing.
implementation_tasks: deepen mitigation ranking so runtime deltas more strongly drive recommendation posture
distinguish inferred, runtime-improved, and runtime-resolved actions in product surfaces
improve residual-risk and confidence language for runtime-backed cases
make the chosen mitigation easier to understand and defend
done_when: runtime evidence more strongly drives mitigation ranking and confidence
operators can see why the chosen action won
runtime-backed cases feel more decision-driving and less merely descriptive

title: Fresh-incident triage quality calibration and evaluation
description: Improve how the product evaluates and calibrates fresh-incident triage quality. The goal is to make fresh incidents more adaptive and more consistent while remaining explicit about lower confidence and bounded support when runtime packs are unavailable.
implementation_tasks: improve fresh-incident triage quality around issue framing, owner routing, next steps, and uncertainty language
add a bounded evaluation harness or reference-case layer where useful
keep fresh-incident outputs from regressing into canned summaries
maintain clear confidence and evidence-posture signals for non-curated incidents
done_when: fresh-incident triage quality is more stable and more adaptive
the product stays honest about lower-confidence non-curated incidents
owner-facing evaluation of fresh-incident quality is easier

title: Post-92 pilot conversion and technical proof checkpoint
description: Close the post-92 phase with a deliberate checkpoint. Confirm the three-outage wedge is easier to pilot, easier to prove, more repo-aware in engineering handoff, more runtime-driven in mitigation posture, and more consistent on fresh incidents while staying bounded and truthful.
implementation_tasks: run the full validation stack across tests, browser flows, demo flow, and packaged-app Docker path
refresh owner-facing docs so the pilot conversion and technical proof posture are current
refresh control docs so the completed backlog is closed cleanly
write a concise checkpoint summary before any broader outage-expansion backlog is activated
done_when: the repo ends the phase with a clean validated checkpoint
owner-facing docs clearly describe the pilot-ready and technical-proof posture of the wedge
control docs are current and ready for the next planning phase
